turns.zh: 我觉得只要在 40 分钟以内我就满意了。这不是我的最好成绩，不是。我想我以前做得更快一点，但快不了多少。而且在我的朋友中，我在阵亡将士纪念日那天并没有赢。我有个朋友实际上比我快了几分钟。但我想澄清一件事，我在网上看到很多关于这个的问题：Murph 挑战有多种做法。有一种是分组模式，你把引体向上、俯卧撑和深蹲组合成几组来做；另一种是不分组模式，你先做 100 个引体向上，然后 200 个俯卧撑，再 300 个深蹲。显然，如果你不分组做，完成 100 个引体向上的时间会更长，因为你在引体向上之间休息的时候，并没有同时做俯卧撑和深蹲。所以我不分组的时间会慢很多。但说到底，首先我认为这是纪念阵亡将士纪念日的好方式。这基本上是墨菲中尉最喜欢的训练之一，我每年阵亡将士纪念日都会尝试做一次。这是一次很好的锻炼。这次我让我的大女儿们和我一起做。我大女儿想要一个负重背心，因为她看到我穿着负重背心做。我不知道一个七岁的孩子是否应该穿着负重背心做引体向上。我当时想，也许我可以给你做一个非常轻的背心，但我觉得这对她来说不太好。所以她基本上做了四分之一 Murph：她跑了四分之一英里，然后做了 25 个引体向上、50 个俯卧撑和 75 个徒手深蹲，然后又跑了四分之一英里，用时 15 分钟，这让我印象深刻。还有我五岁的女儿也是。所以我对此很兴奋。我很高兴我在教他们身体锻炼的价值。我觉得我女儿最开心的一天就是她和我一起去健身房，一口气做很多引体向上。我喜欢她这一点。希望我在教她一些好的东西。

ts.sec: Ideal Exercise Regimen for a Busy Life

turns.en: Given how busy you are, given how much stuff you have going on in your life, what's the perfect exercise regimen for you to keep yourself happy and productive in your main line of work?
turns.zh: 考虑到你有多忙，生活中有多少事情，对你来说，什么样的完美锻炼计划能让你保持快乐并在主要工作中保持高效？

turns.en: Right now I'm focused most of my workouts on fighting, so jiu-jitsu and MMA. But I don't know, maybe if you're professional you can do that every day. I can't. I just get too many bruises and things that you need to recover from. So I do that three to four times a week, and then the other days I just try to do a mix of things like cardio, conditioning, strength building, mobility. So I try to do something physical every day. I try to, unless I'm just so tired that I need to relax. But then I'll still try to go for a walk or something. Even here, I don't know, have you been on the roof yet? No? We'll go on the roof after this. But we designed this building and I put a park on the roof so that way meetings, when I'm just doing a one-on-one or talking to a couple people, I have a very hard time just sitting. I feel like you get super stiff, it feels really bad. But I don't know, being physical is very important to me. I think it's... I do not believe that a being is just a mind. I think we're kind of meant to do things physically, and a lot of the sensations that we feel are connected to that. I think that's a lot of what makes you a human: having that set of sensations and experiences around that, coupled with a mind to reason about them. But I don't know, I think it's important for balance to get out, challenge yourself in different ways, learn different skills, clear your mind.
turns.zh: 目前我的大部分锻炼都集中在格斗上，也就是柔术和综合格斗。但我不确定，如果你是职业选手，也许可以每天都练。我做不到。我只是有太多瘀伤和需要恢复的地方。所以我每周练三到四次，其余的日子我会尝试混合做一些有氧、体能训练、力量训练和柔韧性训练。所以我尽量每天都做点身体活动。我尽量，除非我累到需要放松。但即使那样，我还是会试着去散个步什么的。即使在这里，我不知道，你上过屋顶吗？没有？我们待会儿可以去屋顶。我们设计这栋楼的时候，我在屋顶上建了一个公园，这样开会的时候，当我做一对一或者和几个人谈话时，我很难一直坐着。我觉得你会变得非常僵硬，感觉非常糟糕。但我不确定，身体活动对我来说非常重要。我认为……我不相信一个存在只是头脑。我觉得我们天生就是要做身体上的事情，我们感受到的很多感觉都与此相关。我认为这很大程度上造就了你作为人的本质：拥有围绕这一点的一系列感觉和体验，再加上一个用来推理它们的头脑。但我不确定，我认为为了平衡，走出去、以不同的方式挑战自己、学习不同的技能、理清思绪是很重要的。

ts.sec: Does AI Need a Body for Superintelligence?

turns.en: Do you think AI, in order to become super intelligent, should have a body?
turns.zh: 你认为人工智能为了变得超级智能，应该拥有一个身体吗？

turns.en: It depends on what the goal is. I think there's this assumption in that question that intelligence should be kind of person-like, whereas you can have these greater-than-single-human intelligent organisms like the stock market, which obviously do not have bodies and do not speak a language, and just have their own system. So my guess is there will be limits to what a system that is purely an intelligence can understand about the human condition without having the same not just senses, but like our bodies change as we get older, and we kind of evolve. I think that those very subtle physical changes drive a lot of social patterns and behavior around when you choose to have kids, how you design things around the house. So if the goal is to understand people as much as possible, then trying to model those sensations is probably somewhat important. But I think there's a lot of value that can be created by having intelligence that is separate from that.
turns.zh: 这取决于目标是什么。我认为这个问题里有一个假设，即智能应该是类似人的，但你可以有比单个人类更智能的有机体，比如股票市场，它显然没有身体，也不说语言，只是有自己的系统。所以我的猜测是，一个纯粹智能的系统，在没有相同感官的情况下，能理解的人类状况是有限的——不仅是感官，还有我们的身体随着年龄增长而变化，我们也在进化。我认为那些非常细微的身体变化驱动了很多社会模式和行为，比如你什么时候选择要孩子，你如何设计家里的东西。所以如果目标是尽可能理解人类，那么尝试建模那些感觉可能有些重要。但我认为，拥有与此分离的智能也能创造很多价值。

turns.en: One of the features of being human is that we're mortal, we die. We've talked about AI a lot, about potentially replicas of ourselves. Do you think there will be AI replicas of you and me that persist long after we're gone, that family and ones can talk to?
turns.zh: 作为人类的一个特征是我们终有一死。我们讨论了很多关于人工智能，关于可能存在的我们自己的复制品。你认为会有你我的 AI 复制品在我们去世后长久存在，让家人和亲人可以与之交谈吗？

turns.en: I think we'll have the capacity to do something like that. And I think one of the big questions that we've had to struggle with in the context of social networks is who gets to make that. And my answer to that, in the context of the work that we're doing, is that that should be your choice. I don't think anyone else should be able to create that.
turns.zh: 我认为我们将有能力做类似的事情。我认为在社交网络的背景下，我们不得不挣扎的一个大问题是，谁有权做这件事。而我在我们正在做的工作的背景下给出的答案是，这应该是你自己的选择。我不认为其他任何人应该能够创造它。

turns.en: Should be able to choose to make a Lex spot that people can choose to talk to and get to train that? Yeah, and we've kind of we have this precedent of making some of these calls where I mean someone can create a page for a Lex fan club but you can't create a page and say that you're Lex, yes, right. So I think that this similarly, I think maybe you know someone maybe can make a should be able to make an AI that's a Lex admirer that someone can talk to, but I think it should ultimately your call whether there is a Lex AI.
turns.zh: 人们应该可以选择创建一个“Lex 专区”，让大家可以与之交谈并训练它？是的，我们有过一些先例，比如有人可以创建一个 Lex 粉丝俱乐部页面，但不能创建一个页面声称你就是 Lex，对吧。所以我认为类似地，也许有人可以创建一个“Lex 仰慕者”AI 供人交谈，但最终是否要有一个 Lex AI，应该由你决定。

turns.en: Well, I'm open-sourcing The Lex, so...
turns.zh: 嗯，我正在开源 Lex，所以……

ts.sec: Role of faith in life and work

turns.en: You're a man of faith. What role has faith played in your life and your understanding of the world and your understanding of your own life and your understanding of your work and how your work impacts the world?
turns.zh: 你是一个有信仰的人。信仰在你的生活中扮演了什么角色？它如何影响你对世界的理解、对自己人生的理解、对工作的理解，以及你的工作如何影响世界？

turns.en: Yeah, I think that there's a few different parts of this that are relevant. There's sort of a philosophical part and there's a cultural part. One of the most basic lessons is right at the beginning of Genesis, right? It's like God creates the Earth and creates people and creates people in God's image, and there's the question of what that means. All the only context that you have about God at that point in the Old Testament is that God has created things. So I always thought that one of the interesting lessons from that is that there's a virtue in creating things, whether it's artistic or whether you're building things that are functionally useful for other people. I think that by itself is a good, and that kind of drives a lot of how I think about morality and my personal philosophy around what is a good life. Right? I think it's one where you're helping the people around you and you're being a kind of positive creative force in the world that is helping to bring new things into the world, whether they're amazing other people's kids or just leading to the creation of different things that wouldn't have been possible otherwise. So that's a value for me that matters deeply. And I just love spending time with the kids and seeing that they're trying to impart this value to them. Nothing makes me happier than when I come home from work and I see my daughters building Legos on the table or something. It's like, I did that when I was a kid, right? So many other people were doing this, and I hope you don't lose that spirit when you grow up and you want to just continue building different things no matter what it is. To me, that's a lot of what matters. That's the philosophical piece. I think the cultural piece is just about community and values. That part of things has become a lot more important to me since I've had kids. It's almost autopilot when you're a kid, you're in the kind of being imparted to phase of your life. I didn't really think about religion that much for a while, you know, I was in college before I had kids. And then I think having kids has this way of really making you think about what traditions you want to impart and how you want to celebrate and what balance you want in your life. A bunch of the questions that you've asked and a bunch of the things that we're talking about — just the irony of the curtains coming down as we're talking about mortality once again. Same as last time. This is just that the universe works in... We are definitely living in a simulation. But go ahead. Community, tradition, and the values that faith and religion instill. A lot of the topics that we've talked about today are around how do you balance running a company or different responsibilities with this. I always also just think that it's very grounding to just believe that there is something that is much bigger than you that is guiding things, that amongst other things gives you a bit of humility as you pursue that spirit of creating that you spoke to — creating beauty in the world. And as Dostoevsky said, beauty will save the world.
turns.zh: 是的，我认为这涉及几个不同的方面。有哲学层面，也有文化层面。最基本的教训之一就在《创世记》的开头，对吧？上帝创造天地，创造人，按照自己的形象造人，然后问题就是这到底意味着什么。在《旧约》中，你对上帝的全部了解就是上帝创造了万物。所以我一直觉得其中一个有趣的教训是，创造本身就有一种美德，无论是艺术创作，还是建造对他人有实际用途的东西。我认为这本身就是善，这很大程度上驱动了我对道德的看法，以及我关于什么是美好人生的个人哲学。我认为美好的人生就是帮助身边的人，成为世界上一种积极的创造力量，把新事物带到世界上，无论是别人的孩子，还是创造出原本不可能存在的东西。所以这对我来说是一个非常重要的价值观。我喜欢和孩子们在一起，看到他们也在努力传递这个价值观。没有什么比下班回家看到女儿们在桌上搭乐高更让我开心的了。我小时候也这样做，对吧？很多其他人也这样做，我希望你长大后不要失去那种精神，无论是什么，都想要继续建造不同的东西。对我来说，这很重要。这是哲学部分。我认为文化部分是关于社区和价值观。自从有了孩子，这部分对我来说变得更加重要。当你还是个孩子时，你处于被灌输的阶段，几乎是自动的。我有一段时间没有怎么思考宗教，上大学时，在生孩子之前。然后我觉得有了孩子会让你真正思考你想传递什么传统，你想如何庆祝，以及你生活中想要什么样的平衡。你问的很多问题，我们讨论的很多事情——就在我们谈论死亡的时候，窗帘落下来了，这真是讽刺。和上次一样。这就是宇宙的运行方式……我们绝对活在模拟中。但请继续。社区、传统，以及信仰和宗教灌输的价值观。我们今天讨论的很多话题都是关于如何平衡经营公司或不同责任与这些。我也一直认为，相信有比你更伟大的东西在引导一切，这让人非常踏实，在你追求你所说的创造精神——在世界上创造美——时，这能给你一些谦逊。正如陀思妥耶夫斯基所说，美将拯救世界。

turns.en: Mark, I'm a huge fan of yours. Honored to be able to call you a friend. And I am looking forward to both kicking your ass and you kicking my ass on the mat tomorrow in jiu-jitsu, this incredible sport and art that we both will participate in. Thank you so much for talking today. Thank you for everything you're doing in so many exciting realms of technology and human life. I can't wait to talk to you again in the metaverse. Thank you.
turns.zh: Mark，我是你的超级粉丝。很荣幸能称你为朋友。我期待明天在柔术垫上和你互相切磋，这项我们共同参与的不可思议的运动和艺术。非常感谢你今天的谈话。感谢你在技术和人类生活这么多激动人心的领域所做的一切。我迫不及待想在元宇宙中再次与你交谈。谢谢。

turns.en: Thanks for listening to this conversation with Mark Zuckerberg. To support this podcast, please check out our sponsors in the description. And now let me leave you with some words from Isaac Asimov: "It is change, continuing change, inevitable change, that is the dominant factor in society today. No sensible decision can be made any longer without taking into account not only the world as it is, but the world as it will be." Thank you for listening and hope to see you next time.
turns.zh: 感谢收听本期与 Mark Zuckerberg 的对话。要支持本播客，请查看描述中的赞助商。现在，让我用艾萨克·阿西莫夫的话作为结束：“变化，持续的变化，不可避免的变化，是当今社会的主导因素。任何明智的决策都不能再只考虑世界现在的样子，而不考虑它未来的样子。”感谢收听，希望下次再见。

consensus.en: Open-sourcing AI models at current stage is beneficial for innovation and safety.
consensus.zh: 在当前阶段开源AI模型有利于创新和安全。

consensus.en: Future AI products will involve many specialized agents, not one singular AI.
consensus.zh: 未来的AI产品将包含许多专门的智能体，而非单一的AI。

consensus.en: Mixed reality with Quest 3 at $499 will be a major step toward mainstream adoption.
consensus.zh: 售价499美元的Quest 3混合现实将是迈向主流采用的重要一步。

consensus.en: Scaling current LLMs alone may not lead to AGI; more fundamental breakthroughs needed.
consensus.zh: 仅靠扩展当前大型语言模型可能无法实现AGI，需要更多根本性突破。

consensus.en: AI assistants will help creators and businesses interact with audiences at scale.
consensus.zh: AI助手将帮助创作者和企业大规模地与受众互动。

contrarian.en: Open-source AI is more secure than closed-source due to more scrutiny.
contrarian.zh: 开源AI因受到更多审查而比闭源更安全。

contrarian.en: The biggest AI risk is amplifying known harms, not existential superintelligence.
contrarian.zh: AI最大的风险是放大已知的危害，而非存在性的超级智能。

contrarian.en: Apple's $3,500 Vision Pro may boost demand for Meta's more affordable Quest 3.
contrarian.zh: 苹果3500美元的Vision Pro可能会提升对Meta更实惠的Quest 3的需求。

contrarian.en: Text-based social networks like Twitter have untapped potential for billion users.
contrarian.zh: 像Twitter这样的文本社交网络有潜力达到十亿用户，但尚未实现。

contrarian.en: Censorship should focus on clear harms, not disputed facts or opinions.
contrarian.zh: 审查应关注明确的危害，而非有争议的事实或观点。
